0467057c-543d-4a47-a30e-3e10951dea4e.content:
This is a label. In Play Mode, it appears as a player option.

Labels can do more than this; they can also contain code. But let's keep it simple for now.

137fd37b-86ce-4a02-8e8d-250172a3f0eb.content: The wide image (your scene's background) is an element cover. To add one, drag an image asset from the Assets section (left sidebar) and drop it into the element.

145815ed-47d6-4eb9-9d58-9b88c47b1816.content:
Welcome! This tutorial will guide you through creating a visual novel in Arcweave.

For broader Arcweave fundamentals, check out the Interactive Fiction example in the project templates.

36b0f4e4-2344-489f-bca7-e7d44ec26c26.content:
Notice how the audio changes here. The school audio stops and the festival one starts.

Click on the attached audio assets, to see their settings.

39c8d2fd-4e11-4a2d-971b-c972c7771dd0.content: Creating a connection is intuitive: drag from any edge of the source element and drop into the target.

3fd33728-d249-4683-a546-30bfcff6e6da.content:
The character images are components. To attach a component, drag it from the Components section (also in the left sidebar) into the element.

To change the attached components' order, simply drag and drop them relative to each other.

424bb5db-dd45-417b-bdb7-c4a838760078.content: This is your story's starting element. Double click it to edit its title or content. (In rich text, too!)

4b662f1d-bdad-47c9-aad0-77cf6c313047.content:
This is a label. In Play Mode, it appears as a player option.

Labels can do more than this; they can also contain code. But let's keep it simple for now.

660685e7-83df-4ae2-83a3-856ec15d614e.content: Notice the images in the element below.

8fadad9b-50d9-486d-bb86-f4723fdc939d.content:
To begin, press the Play (▶️) button in the top menu to enter Play Mode.

If you’ve played other Arcweave projects before, you’ll already be familiar with Play Mode.

This project, however, makes use of the Style Editor to create the classic visual novel look, instead of relying on Arcweave’s default CSS.

9db8fd48-acc7-44fa-b8d2-430f8b45d78b.content:
To change a character's emotion and facial expression, you need to create a new component.

Set the desired image asset as the new component's cover.

Notice how this project uses different components for each characters default and smile expression.

b36627c6-29f3-4971-980c-a8d45c547f57.content:
This is a label. In Play Mode, it appears as a player option.

Labels can do more than this; they can also contain code. But let's keep it simple for now.

d23b9574-b3ae-4ca6-aaec-639879be8009.content: This is a connetion. Connect elements to create the flow of your story.

dea777b9-444a-44f6-b5c3-dee7a72e14ca.content:
Notice how the audio changes here. The school audio stops and the home one starts.

Click on the attached audio assets, to see their settings.

e31f8c73-9d3a-43f2-a009-04a569b1c06b.content:
Image Credits
Characters by Sutemo

Music Credits
Bg music by AuthorSeo

ef914b97-7f4c-4269-8a6a-3c7c73b20373.content:
Click on the attached component to edit it.

You can change its name and cover (as well as add attributes, but let's leave this for another tutorial).

f1bddc47-bc2c-4942-842d-5359ac1fb039.content:
Add audio to your elements so it plays during Play Mode.

To do this, drag an audio asset from the Assets section in the left sidebar and drop it into the element.

Click on the attached audio asset to set its behavior: play once, loop, or stop.

075df522-6a99-4c26-a4c8-d8c91fc3975c.content: Frank: If you'd cleaned the wound with water or covered it with napkins, it could've introduced different types of infections to the wound.

07f149ff-7b50-4a78-be69-d200f2c422e6.content: Frank: Hey, the wound is still actively bleeding!! You don’t want to wash it with water yet, so you don’t need to get water.

0b751d7e-7752-4c4c-a989-5feca13e5267.content: Frank: Just get my bag over there.

0bd61532-189d-44eb-bf38-edfea9672dd9.content: Frank: I know, I know, but god, it hurts… so much more now. It's bleeding through the wrappings.

0dc8856e-915d-4721-91f1-751ac5a93006.content: Frank: Just get my bag over there.

0ee54dbb-ac8f-47be-a6aa-8c5db6737ada.content: [Name]: What do you normally eat. then?

126b988e-de6d-4ac7-bb37-8904d96075e1.content: There is a lot you can do.

15fe7685-9090-4750-9192-81ca573d84eb.content: You move the chair over to Frank.

17699794-7c76-4498-9ba6-8a18d0022c47.content: Frank: Honestly, mostly it just itches. The doctors said I got lucky.

1884e9c7-6a24-4fcb-b7f3-d1d0d990e855.content: Frank: These gauze rolls, though, should be cleaner.

1a17b6ab-639e-481a-bef0-92deb0473239.content: You see someone who is tired. You have to assess whether they are just sleep deprived, or actually suffering from alcohol poisoning.
1a17b6ab-639e-481a-bef0-92deb0473239.title: Decoy

1bc871cb-4ccd-4f39-8a1b-f86d1851911f.content: You got a cup of hot water.

1be8758c-59f1-4573-897d-387fbca0ff98.content: What kind of person comes to a party just to drink water?

1d52a7cb-6bc1-4479-a812-bd452ce1d4ad.content: The timer starts now. you can interact with your phone to call 911.

1dcd98ca-942a-4387-a492-2b9e3709012f.content: 911 Operator: We’re sending a unit over right now. Please stay in place.

2417cc0d-d6fb-46a1-a687-376405f9dab1.content: Frank: Alright, can you bring that over here?

243dab40-e347-4cbb-9ec2-dee66776f3bc.content: Frank: What? What do you want me to say? Thanks for the millionth time?

264b7f5f-19ed-431d-b8fe-0512c0850262.content: Frank: Oh, you know, as my training was going on, my parents felt like it’d be better if I got a place closer to the hospital.

2d3ee6a5-b0d6-4f0e-9d83-ea74ab61a63e.content: [Name]: Did you see how much they drank?

30611252-18d7-4d79-a1d1-a6244fa38792.content: Frank: Alright, you’re going to need to wrap the wound. It's a good thing I had those gauze rolls handy.

324b6d77-3896-467d-8687-edaae87748b3.content: Liv: Oh god. They’re not breathing. What do I do, she’s not breathing!!

32767a7c-9281-46ec-b409-e5098882aa0b.content: Frank: Oh crap. Okay, it’s important to stay calm and act carefully.
32767a7c-9281-46ec-b409-e5098882aa0b.title: Maya's Invitation

35533a31-2b6e-4cf2-9bf9-a904836fc398.content:
You check your phone for the address.

[Name]: Alright, on my left… 8, 10… ah. 2012 Chimney Rock Road.
35533a31-2b6e-4cf2-9bf9-a904836fc398.title: Maya's Invitation

355f7b6a-adc7-4eb6-b4fa-510066809f9e.content: Onlooker: Come on, Vic, let’s get you outside.

3647681a-f09b-4c7e-88da-37ec536b2710.content: 911 Operator: Alright, keep doing that. How much alcohol did this person drink?

36c07e05-0b06-4962-9e74-9f65e2f23734.content: 911 Operator: Alright, thank you. What is your current location?

372748f0-158b-4417-b930-cadd7aba3cbc.content: Frank: Be careful with that knife, though. You’re not supposed to remove things when they pierce the skin.

39d35cf1-d157-4104-9e1d-7ea75daa1e42.content: Frank: Keeping the knife in actually stops the foot from bleeding out faster.

3a7710bf-eece-48d9-a816-e66750fcb1d2.content: You get the supplies and unlock the Inventory. You can now access the inventory, seeing the medical supplies as well as your cell phone. You can now go back to Frank, who is now sitting down on the floor, pressing his foot.

3d3c392d-0440-417a-a3d8-3a7d48f64e26.content: Onlooker: Whatever. We need to get her outside...

413e9339-8aff-4f38-8cc0-c5e39a29906b.content: Liv: I, um I’ve never been trained, but.

414c5a3b-3caa-4682-a490-670424170b7f.content: Liv: Oh my god. What do I do???

430251d4-6008-48ca-a7db-88465f8fdc1e.content: Liv: What? The police? Won't we get in trouble? I don't turn 21 until-

440bfc44-bdc4-47c2-a6a4-5f31afb842a2.content: You can now click on the chair. You can also inspect the wound.

470aac9f-768c-41b3-8595-3d613fa6afb6.content: Vicky: Since when were there two of you…?

490af16f-2603-43e6-85ae-f418e94cc070.content: WooaAH! That's got... A bit of BUZZ!

4afe38e3-9ad5-4e46-8b64-99ef752dc00b.content: I took the bus. Thank you. I’ll be fine.

4b2aeb83-46f8-4b69-b205-6eabd4db2b26.content: [Name]: Did you see how much they drank?

4be3044e-e7a5-416d-a289-6683a492dd2d.content: Player: I’m at a party, and someone just passed out. They drank a lot of alcohol. They’ve been slurring speech since a few minutes ago. They just stopped breathing.

4cb6e722-9a5a-44a6-9026-08fcc10f14e9.content: [Name]: You know, this is my first time seeing your new place. How's it been, here?

4d849e11-d08a-4f3d-ab21-c04da9330ede.content: You accidentally knock hard into the knife, causing it to dig deeper into the foot.

53e25832-31a9-4a2b-99cf-134eb6f488df.content: Honestly, water could be good. Or some tea.

54ccd07e-7b3d-435c-aa0e-b3f781f99118.content: Frank: Because I’ll say it! Thanks, [Name]! Now, let’s move on.

558aeac1-0f1d-4f0c-b0a6-aed2906dc724.content: Now that you’re outside, you and Liv drop Vicky on the patio.

59bcf675-fbb4-4096-8778-07bbb34c87af.content: Onlooker (Liv): Her hands are cold… That’s not normal.

5d00bdb7-a34f-49e5-ad42-a7373f72f7f0.content: There is a lot you can do.

5f927cbb-97f3-47bc-bd2d-4e1b2560d4ab.content: I… I only had like… like, four cups.

60bf6b98-6c55-4d0e-823b-6b66a9b0276d.content: Make sure you check your surroundings and ensure they’re safe. Look around!

622ddedf-fe24-4b50-a390-64d8d7bd45bd.content:
Player: Chest compressions. You need to push hard into their chest.

Here, I can show you.

628acbcc-c916-452f-8a83-4f829dbd4995.content: Frank is now pressing on his foot with one hand, and leaning against the chair with the other, still sitting on the floor.

642a5cfc-4623-4293-8b31-c4b4351ebd70.content: You see someone who is tired. You have to assess whether they are just sleep deprived, or actually suffering from alcohol poisoning.
642a5cfc-4623-4293-8b31-c4b4351ebd70.title: Alcoholism

67146f49-df84-4d89-9203-40a46b0c8fb3.content: Frank: I’ll… keep the pressure on my foot.

6831a0a4-00e4-49bf-a3b2-c210e7979b22.content: Onlooker: Oh god, Vicky... You really can't keep doing this.

6869ccd3-7024-4783-bc22-def7dea947c0.content: Frank: Wait, no - dabbing the wound with napkins is dangerous. They can fall apart and stick to the injury.

6a7bac0f-4fd3-40df-b9b7-9aebb7ead273.content: Friend "Hold up! That's not right!! It's 2012 Chimney Rock Road!"

6b077b2a-3bef-421d-b073-414d6cc80816.content: You mix the tea into the hot water.

6c00464c-e3f3-4462-8ede-ea8c2f4ed096.title: Untitled component

6e551804-2622-4b8d-a0ac-93e09986d429.content: Frank: I mean, I have a knife in my foot, but… I need to relax. Don’t want to go into shock or anything.

6eaff6ac-2fdb-437a-a351-e3980f5ca749.content: Frank: Geez, stop looking at it! Just get the bandages already!

714a2e99-b0e7-4b7f-b4fd-7fcebc821e05.content: Frank: Okay, great. Calm down, it’s fine. Ok. Can you get my first aid kit? It’s in my bag.
714a2e99-b0e7-4b7f-b4fd-7fcebc821e05.title: Jake's Invitation

7250a697-9722-4ab8-80ef-b34b22da5f54.content: Liv: Oh… oh god… ok… I’ll try my best.

72ba4268-a7ac-45e0-9ec4-11928379a737.content: Driver Friend: Oh, we’re here. Wow, it got dark out fast.

73185b22-b435-4677-8710-7f4bd24fe369.content: Player: "My friend dropped a knife on his foot at his home. I’m with him and wrapped it temporarily, but it needs medical attention."

76e9a5cb-971a-48b2-9de0-495ebc864bb0.content: You give the person the hot tea.

775cfc87-42c9-4c4e-a788-d09403beac48.content:
Those… big old red ones… heh.

(They're pointing at a punch bowl with wine bottles next to it. )

77a2eb09-585b-4fa3-96f1-1d5634414411.content: Player: Calm down. There's an amnesty clause. As long as you're helping, you won't get in trouble.

7886024f-0500-45ed-ba40-f917f91309ff.content: You make note of the broken glass from your fallen water cup.
7886024f-0500-45ed-ba40-f917f91309ff.title: Street Festival

78e0ef60-8665-4ec9-b7f9-e5c4d963a156.content: You got a cup of hot water.

7b2a946e-bb94-4d2a-8a56-4d4caf3c294a.content: You’re at the party. Everyone appears to be having a good time. It’s late spring. It’s warm outside despite it being night.

7ef975df-c200-4880-bfa0-de3375ec51b7.content: You enter your friend's house. You pour yourself a glass of water, like a force of habit. Frank goes back to a cutting board, with various vegetables chopped up on the side. There are several knives hanging above the stove.

81ca4304-78c7-470d-8b99-6d5a2eb70edc.content: You're in the car with your friends. Frank is here. He has a cast on his foot, now.
81ca4304-78c7-470d-8b99-6d5a2eb70edc.title: Level 1 Start

83523415-cabe-4d86-a4e0-cd38ddd0a064.content: [Name]: Ok, there you go. How did you get here? Did you come with anyone?

842d9061-b68d-45c8-9f65-3cd4ae422b5e.content: [Name]: It was an accident! I’m sorry!

84b19888-ff99-41b5-84e4-edf4e52c3d19.content:
Flavor Text

You remember this is called "recovery position".

867b2073-485c-4c59-8213-6d9233640544.content: Frank: OW, DAMMIT! Don't touch the knife!!

894903cc-624b-4cc2-9170-e974e93bfee8.content: Friend "Come on, ow! How did you even get here? It's 2012 Chimney Rock Road!!"

8a30a4b0-4427-4805-852c-2a2d2b00da35.content: You give the person the hot water.

8a7b4525-e1fd-47e7-a14e-a2129d0f8e99.content: [Name]: Man, how did you afford this place?

8ad165a7-818b-4f16-9528-4376c89d7406.content: You mix the tea into the hot water.

8c2b3480-f266-4bd1-9695-afa7a0ece06f.content: You and the Onlooker each help Vicky stand.

906fcd94-d1eb-4e43-9b38-5f9b04b7eec2.content: [Name]: So, what are you cooking up right now?

94c9601f-9d07-4292-af4e-51791102419f.content: Frank: A vegetable soup. I feel like I haven't been eating enough vegetables.

94e225c5-6383-4516-b293-037a25c5c154.content: Player: I’m going to call 911. We don’t know if their condition will worsen.

95f3587c-a200-4d83-81d3-cb031c1b82b9.content: Frank: Well, I did a lot more cooking back in high school, but I've been just getting fast food a lot for the past year. The freshman dorm didn't have any convenient stove, anyhow.

962d91ed-0391-4eb6-85b9-75a9cb1c7903.content: Frank: More sterile than the napkins, at any rate.

9b399fff-577d-4518-8854-86f9aa2220f7.content: 911 Operator: This is 911, what is your emergency?

9b7ff1de-af2a-49ef-a25a-66501efa4477.content: Player:There. You got that rhythm? You just need to keep doing that.

9bcd7db2-fb3e-4d42-bd6a-5562d25a42ba.content: Frank: I mean, I have a knife in my foot, but… I need to relax. Don’t want to go into shock or anything.

a2269b29-e0d1-42c8-9b76-67060018ed14.content: Frank: Can you, um, bring a chair over?

a3a7adf3-1a1b-444d-8f02-01a2a440cd8b.content:
Onlooker: I think they had like, 6 cups?

I haven’t been watching her constantly, though.

a6b83872-84ea-431e-8228-eb73c77a2359.content:
Flavor Text

Oh no. Your friends drove you here. You don’t know the address.

a8e8eee6-76df-4ed4-bbf0-8ac65ec5877b.content: Based on your assessment, how will you help?

a8e9a04f-949d-430d-9de0-4ff88182353c.content: They start coughing a lot. They look pale...

aa31e639-f2e6-4cec-aad6-bd3ca242e92e.content: You investigate the knife wound. It’s fully through the foot. Blood is coming out of the top and on the floor underneath.

aa7603c6-9af8-409b-90a2-3be46d2c0058.content: Frank: Wait, before you do anything, mind the glass there. I don't want us both to get stabbed in the foot.
aa7603c6-9af8-409b-90a2-3be46d2c0058.title: Cozy Evening

ab261dea-d34d-48a2-a0b3-602fe16bd0ec.content: 911-Operator: "When calling for help, please provide important details, so I can properly assess your situation. What’s the injury, specifically?"

abb0f32e-b35f-4271-b631-5f0860fddd42.content: Your name is [Name]. Your best friend is Frank, a guy you met in freshman year at a chess club. You're majoring in different things, so you don't see each other very often. Today, he's invited you to his house for lunch.

ae897635-78bd-4fc4-9cd8-aeed10832b66.content:
Flavor Text

You remember something about putting the victim on their side.

af47edb6-9737-487f-b9b6-b5638d07e678.content: Onlooker: Oh, I know them. They never drink.

b4be1236-9e81-4344-81eb-949e9b345c6b.content: Frank: There should be plenty of medical supplies in there for this.

b626231f-e929-4430-a614-71c495290d67.content: [Name]: How’s your medical training going, anyhow?

b7a6623c-b4f7-4c04-97d7-7676e1ce0991.content:
Liv looks down at Vicky.

She's stopped breathing.

ba2697a0-0367-442b-9101-038f069bfd6b.content: Frank: I need to keep pressure on the wound, but my arm’s getting sore.

bbfa3db7-45f9-46ce-b8e5-4a3902901719.content: Frank: It’s been tough. They really try to make sure you’re ready for anything.

c0914a0d-a9dc-433c-81af-78bd13360ca3.content: Frank: It’s nice! I haven’t been able to switch all the mail over yet, though. I keep getting letters addressed to the old tenants.

c10eb371-8ca2-4b34-bfa6-9c733a1ca628.content:
You check Vicky's pulse.

She doesn't have one.

c4796d0a-083a-43eb-8d2b-5f97231fc55b.content: You then finish wrapping. Absent-mindedly, you knock into the knife, causing it to dig into the foot more.

c89d3f0a-375e-4790-96b1-e371359b6f0d.content: [Name]: I don’t think you should be driving if you’re sleep deprived.

ca4fb4d4-77a0-4403-a2f5-2b829be59aef.content:
You remember learning this at a summer camp, once.

They taught you the best way to keep time was to think about 

a catchy rhythm with around 100 BPM.

ca8446d8-6bdb-499e-a49c-273f159548fa.content: Driver Friend: Glad you’re feeling good enough to go out.

cab1c5b6-3598-466e-b78b-e86e50eb0c46.content: [Name]: I wish my parents got me a place like this.

cb35db92-3c07-41bf-9c45-580ad6b891b4.content:
Welcome to the Vital Minute!
Choose your name.
cb35db92-3c07-41bf-9c45-580ad6b891b4.title: Friday Afternoon

d070b02b-b689-4956-9c44-a16b4de2cdf6.content: I had this project I wanted to get done before the party today.

d3153f62-fb30-4b46-af95-69301cc571f4.content: Player: "My friend’s foot is bleeding even though I wrapped it. Please send help!!"

d359777c-a124-4604-9062-b82f9462cf9d.content: I… Drank what? I had a… one of those… glasses, like ten minutes ago.

d4cc204b-19c7-4289-ac50-1917bd32633e.content: Frank: …Sometimes, yeah. But I guess after enough exposure, you grow desensitized to the sight of-

d560c822-c3d4-46e6-b8d8-a5e2bc72c028.content:
End sequence where we hear sirens and see Vicky get carried away by EMS. Liv goes with her.

The sounds of the sirens cause partygoers to panic and scatter. After a bit, Frank stumbles out of the house, a little tipsy. You’re still sitting on the porch.

dd571a37-d393-4fd1-af4e-06158b4d9447.content: 911-Operator: "We’re sending a unit over right now. Please stay in place.

ddc81088-660b-4114-aa9a-21996054bf4d.content: Driver Friend: Bet you’re glad [Name] was there to save ya, huh?

de925341-5861-41e7-b508-897c401aa90c.content: They start spitting out the alcohol and vomiting on the floor.

df1a4015-2832-414c-b9af-8563f42ff8da.content: You give the person the hot water.

e38ac378-7459-4c67-93c7-ba4be049631c.content: Player: I’m not sure exactly, but it was more than 8 shots. I’m not sure how strong the alcohol is here.

e4531320-4418-404a-b9e9-d54afef0142c.content: Now that they're feeling better, you decide to go check on the other person you saw.

e5499f5b-b60b-4752-976a-9f257afef06a.content: Onlooker: In all my years of knowing them, I’ve never seen them drink even once.

e5f7f3ab-471b-42ad-b207-9b1e0035c10a.content: I didn’t drink anything. I don’t trust these punch bowls anyway.

e67886eb-87cf-495f-bc93-44f536f20392.content: Onlooker: Oh god, Vicky! What the heck did you give her?

eb19b1f4-0cf1-48d7-b2f7-c4c219a3342e.content: I pulled an all-nighter to finish it.

ebed1e6d-b16a-4b3a-8ccf-8615e6103f1c.content:
What the heck is this? I already told you I don't drink.

I don't think I want your help anymore.

ee290ed0-dd96-4d27-b019-089731e7bec2.content:
You have to assess whether they are just sleep deprived, or actually suffering from alcohol poisoning.

Someone has just walked over.
ee290ed0-dd96-4d27-b019-089731e7bec2.title: Vicky

ef7710b0-9aa7-4fd9-adfc-e96a4503b051.content: Luckily, you’re outside, so you just have to look to the right, and the house number is there. Then, look to the left, to see the street sign.

f0175fba-75a1-4cb2-8bc1-8551e68a70b8.content:
Well that could've gone better. You can't help a person who doesn't want help, though.

A little ways away at the party, you see someone fall to the ground and start giggling.

They look exhausted.

f1571a5e-abc0-4b4f-9302-46d2bb9f96f0.content: Frank: Usually that kind of injury means you lose the whole foot.

f2284647-5901-402f-9f18-96af7bcfb32e.content: Vicky: Hrrgh… I don’t wanna… Ugh… My head… hehe… Livvy..

f252ac82-4648-4d41-89f4-04799795c0b5.content: Based on your assessment, how will you help?

f28349fa-f30e-40b2-8672-0426a7ee8ecd.content: Suddenly, one of the knives falls down and impales your friend’s foot. You stand up with a jolt, knocking your water glass onto the ground.

f46f5121-88de-4522-98dc-634636069fec.content:
Frank

Oh, hello there! Come on in! I was almost done making the food.

f4b48678-44bd-476d-965e-c6a6032f6b57.content: Your friends go to party in some side room, so you can’t find them. Cue the awkward moment when you’re at the party and you don’t actually know anyone.

f565f01d-d2d5-4745-a153-4a6c9029e07b.content:
You have to assess whether they are just sleep deprived, or actually suffering from alcohol poisoning.

Someone has just walked over.

f77d2647-faa5-4ec5-b9cd-a1040808f9e4.content: [Name]: I was just going to get some water to rinse the wound.

f7cb21fb-d8e8-449e-8772-a1679440ec94.content: You look closer at the wound. Perhaps there was something in that medical bag that can help here?

f8791b7e-3c53-418f-9c6f-777ae4d65a5e.content: Vicky vomits and passes out, collapsing on the floor. Liv catches them.

f8fa5ace-c61b-4b8d-9f00-d5d85e54f488.content:
Suddenly, you see two people who appear very exhausted. They both appear to have trouble walking. You don't know who they are.

Who do you help?

fa52f960-9711-4abf-8101-d1fe0592c7df.content: Liv is holding Vicky steady on her side.

fabf46c1-a7ff-4460-84f4-ec89e33087e8.content: Player: I already checked. I have her friend giving CPR right now.

fac99888-f592-465d-9b89-b40d5656b804.content: Player: I’m putting her on her side so she doesn’t choke on her vomit.

fadf0695-a237-4b38-9a6b-f54ade8bdc0f.content: You give the person the hot tea.
